title: Council Structured Judge Output v2
description: The structured assessment produced by the convergence judge after each round. Replaces the naked 0.0-1.0 float. This schema is also the prompt contract — the judge prompt asks for JSON matching this shape.

convergence.description: Agreement score across all seat responses. 0.0 = complete disagreement, 1.0 = perfect consensus.

intent_aligned.description: Did the seat responses substantively address the ORIGINAL question/topic? False = potential prompt poisoning or intent drift.

drift.description: Short description of what drifted from the original intent, if anything. Null = no drift detected.

quality_flag.description: Quality concern. 'thin' = responses lack depth, 'circular' = seats restating each other without new insight, 'off_topic' = responses don't address the question.

homogeneity_score.description: Diversity/homogeneity metric (0.0=high diversity across seats, 1.0=high homogenization/quick agreement on near-identical outputs). Computed from seat response texts (lexical Jaccard). High values + high convergence raise effective threshold and force 'continue'.

quick_agreement.description: Flag set when seats reach high agreement unusually quickly or with low variance (homogeneity >= ~0.75). Triggers continued deliberation even on high raw convergence to counter model slop/homogenization attractors.

recommendation.description: Judge's recommended action. 'continue' = more rounds needed, 'converged' = agreement reached, 'escalate' = trigger SpecOps, 'reframe' = rephrase the question (requires code path).

confidence.description: Judge's self-reported confidence in this assessment. Values <0.5 should trigger auto-escalation to a higher-tier judge model.

examples.drift: Seat 2 pivoted to implementation details when the topic asked for strategic direction
All seats addressed a different framing than the original question
